reasoning: I'm not voting for BJP because I suddenly trust them — I don't. But I can't vote TMC anymore, and that's what matters today. Everything I believed about Mamata's government — that the schemes meant something, that women were being protected — fell apart when I learned that rural girls had to wait fifty-five days for help while the system looked the other way, and then a court had to force action on fake jobs that had already ruined thousands of lives. My mother's aunt's friend is one of those women. The welfare money helped my family, but if the people in charge are protecting men who assault women and stealing opportunities meant for ordinary people, then what does Kanyashree even mean? I'm casting this vote angry and heartbroken, not hopeful.
primary_drivers: The sexual assault cases in villages and perceived TMC protection/delay of justice — made personal through my mother's students
Repeated systemic corruption (teacher recruitment, job scam) showing the government is fundamentally rotten
Loss of faith that welfare schemes protect anyone when the system itself shields criminals and the powerful
